problem_spec.summary: Given a prime $p$ and integers $x,y$ with prescribed relative $p$-adic valuations, determine $v_p$ of expressions built from $x,y$ (especially $v_p(x+y)$ when the valuations are unequal, and $v_p(xy)$ / $v_p(x/y)$ when defined).
givens: Family default: $v_p(x)\ne v_p(y)$ (so the valuation of $x+y$ is determined exactly as the minimum).
ask: Compute $v=v_p(x+y)$ (and in variants: $v_p(xy)$ or $v_p(x/y)$ when defined) under the declared valuation relations.

parameter(s).name: relation_mode
parameter(s).purpose: controls whether $v_p(x+y)$ is exactly determined or only lower-bounded

invariant(s).name: valuation_additivity
invariant(s).statement: For nonzero integers, $v_p(xy)=v_p(x)+v_p(y)$ and $v_p(x/y)=v_p(x)-v_p(y)$ when $y\mid x$ in $\mathbb{Z}_{(p)}$.

essential_assumption(s): In the default mode, the instance enforces $v_p(x)\ne v_p(y)$ so that $v_p(x+y)$ is uniquely determined.
When using the equal-valuation mode, the instance must provide enough auxiliary information (e.g., $v_p(x/p^t + y/p^t)$) if an exact value beyond the lower bound is desired.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Under the condition $v_p(x)\ne v_p(y)$, the valuation of the sum is forced to be the minimum, hence uniquely determined; $v_p$ itself is uniquely defined for each nonzero integer.

technique(s).name: valuation_vp_definition
technique(s).purpose: encode divisibility by prime powers as an exponent
outputs: the integer $v_p(n)$ such that $p^{v_p(n)}\mid n$ but $p^{v_p(n)+1}\nmid n$

technique(s).name: compare_vp_on_both_sides
technique(s).purpose: compare valuations to deduce contradictions or to isolate unknown exponents
outputs: inequalities/equalities of integers coming from valuations

theorem(s).name: exact_division_arguments
theorem(s).role: translate "$p^t\mid n$" into "$v_p(n)\ge t$" and vice versa

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Divisibility by prime powers is controlled by a single integer exponent; valuation rules convert multiplicative structure into additive bookkeeping.

critical_trick(s): Factor out the smaller power of $p$ from $x$ and $y$ and use that $p$ does not divide the remaining unit-sum when valuations are unequal.
Use additivity of $v_p$ on products to simplify multiplicative expressions.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: Core integer arithmetic: gcd, lcm, modular exponentiation scaffolding.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: Optional symbolic/number theory utilities: factorint, primerange, mod_inverse, crt; useful for verification tasks.

import_hint(s): from sympy import factorint, crt, mod_inverse

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Validate that the produced object(s) satisfy all defining congruences/divisibility/Diophantine constraints for randomly sampled parameters within the declared domain.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Cross-check the main result via an alternative computation (e.g., brute force for small bounds, or an independent formula such as factorization vs. valuation, or CRT reconstruction vs. direct search).

task(s).name: vp_rule_random_check
task(s).reason: Randomly sample small primes and integers, compute $v_p$ by repeated division, and verify the stated product/sum rules (especially the unequal-valuation sum rule).
